options.label: Mock-only for this phase
options.rationale: Maximizes delivery predictability; real provider hookup deferred to optional feature.

options.label: Real OIDC now, Vendor MFA later
options.rationale: Balances enterprise readiness with reduced external dependency risk.

options.label: Full real OIDC + Vendor MFA now
options.rationale: Most complete but highest schedule risk due external integration dependencies.

clarifications.prompt: 第二階段驗收是否必須接通真實 Corporate OIDC 與 Vendor MFA？
clarifications.rationale: Auth integration depth changes acceptance scope and sequencing for F05/F15.
clarifications.recommendationSummary: Lock one explicit phase gate now to prevent auth implementation churn.

blockingIssues.details: Spec allows mock auth for MVP but also defines full real auth integration. Acceptance depth is not explicitly locked.
blockingIssues.title: Authentication Scope for Phase-2 Acceptance

options.label: Keep 25k RPS target for phase-2 gate
options.rationale: Aligns with documented headroom target and infrastructure sizing.

options.label: Use lower staged target now, ramp later
options.rationale: Reduces immediate risk while preserving measurable progress.

options.label: Defer hard RPS gate to post-MVP
options.rationale: Focuses phase-2 on functional completeness only.

clarifications.prompt: 第二階段是否要以 25k RPS 作為硬性驗收門檻？
clarifications.rationale: Capacity gate strictness changes delivery risk and release readiness criteria.
clarifications.recommendationSummary: Adopt staged capacity gates unless business requires immediate 25k RPS certification.

blockingIssues.details: Throughput assumptions are explicit but may diverge from actual usage and environment limits; staged benchmarking policy is not locked.
blockingIssues.title: Capacity Target Risk Without Staged Validation Policy

options.label: Object storage only
options.rationale: Aligns with scale, security, presigned upload, and backup strategy in §12.6.

options.label: DB base64 only
options.rationale: Simpler MVP implementation but conflicts with large-scale architecture and storage separation.

options.label: Dual mode by environment
options.rationale: Technically possible but introduces dual source-of-truth and legacy-like maintenance overhead.

clarifications.prompt: MVP 的檔案與圖片儲存唯一來源要採哪個？
clarifications.rationale: Current requirement text defines two competing canonical storage paths and size limits.
clarifications.recommendationSummary: Choose object storage as the only source-of-truth, and remove DB base64 path from runtime requirements.

blockingIssues.details: §9 allows base64-in-DB (<=500KB) for MVP, while §12.6 mandates object storage presigned flow with <=2MB and thumbnails. This is a direct source-of-truth conflict for upload architecture.
blockingIssues.title: Image/File Storage Path Conflict (DB base64 vs Object Storage)

decisions.optionLabel: Real OIDC now, Vendor MFA later
Full real OIDC + Vendor MFA now

answers.response: Core: Mock-only for this phase | Excluded: Real OIDC now, Vendor MFA later, Full real OIDC + Vendor MFA now

decisions.optionLabel: Keep 25k RPS target for phase-2 gate
Use lower staged target now, ramp later
Defer hard RPS gate to post-MVP

answers.response: Core: Use lower staged target now, ramp later | Excluded: Keep 25k RPS target for phase-2 gate, Defer hard RPS gate to post-MVP
Core: Object storage only | Excluded: DB base64 only, Dual mode by environment

assumptions: No prior clarifications are locked; all decision gates below remain open.
Stage-1 backend domain behavior and existing OpenAPI baseline remain valid unless explicitly extended here.
Phase-2 delivery is planned as: runnable MVP first, then production hardening as optional feature-flagged extensions.
Frontend and backend remain in one monorepo (Cargo workspace + `frontend/` PNPM workspace) sharing generated API client artifacts.
External enterprise dependencies (OIDC/MFA/HR template) may require cross-team coordination outside this repository.
Requirement text in this prompt is treated as highest-priority source when it conflicts with older docs.

acceptanceSignals: `make dev`, `make dev-deps`, `make dev-full`, `make dev-seed`, and `make dev-down` run as documented.
Fresh clone can start dependency services and run backend/frontend locally without manual DB/cache/queue installs.
Seed command creates coverage data for all major UI states (order lifecycle, compliance states, disputes, anomalies).
excludedApproaches: Relying on unmanaged developer-local services or cloud-only dependencies for normal development.
Ad-hoc startup scripts without Makefile entrypoints.
executionConstraints: Provide `docker-compose.yml` and `docker-compose.full.yml` with PostgreSQL, Valkey, NATS JetStream, and MinIO.
Commit `.env.development`; keep `.env.local` as gitignored override.
Include `scripts/setup-dev.sh` checks for toolchain readiness and Docker daemon availability.
features.name: Local Development Stack and Seed Baseline
features.rationale: Every downstream feature depends on reproducible local execution and realistic test data.
features.summary: Bootstrap one-command local development and seeded data coverage.

acceptanceSignals: Full migration up/down cycle succeeds in CI on clean PostgreSQL.
Schema includes required phase-2 tables, timestamps, constraints, and indexes.
Monetary, enum/check, and append-only invariants are validated by integration tests.
excludedApproaches: Manual schema edits in runtime environments.
FLOAT/DOUBLE usage for monetary values.
executionConstraints: Use PostgreSQL 16+ and `sqlx migrate` only for schema evolution.
Apply one global ID strategy from ISS-006 across core tables.
Store monetary amounts as `BIGINT` in minor units; include UTC `created_at`/`updated_at` fields.
Keep `order_timeline_events` and `audit_events` append-only.
features.name: PostgreSQL Schema and Migration Foundation
features.rationale: Persistent correctness is prerequisite for all API and UI workflows.
features.summary: Define audited relational schema and migration lifecycle.

acceptanceSignals: Core domain flows pass against real PostgreSQL using testcontainers.
`sqlx` compile-time query checks pass in CI.
Production path no longer depends on in-memory persistence adapters.
excludedApproaches: Mock-only DB integration tests.
Runtime fallback to in-memory writes when DB operations fail.
executionConstraints: Implement repository adapters with explicit SQL (`sqlx`), not opaque ORM-generated SQL.
Use `sqlx::PgPool` and explicit transaction boundaries for order/payroll state transitions.
Remove legacy in-memory repository path from production runtime.
features.name: Rust Persistence Integration
features.rationale: Domain logic must execute on durable storage before frontend rollout.
features.summary: Bind existing domain services to SQL-backed repositories and transactions.

acceptanceSignals: New endpoints exist and are authorized: employee orders list, payroll summary, vendor documents (POST/GET), menu-item status PATCH.
OpenAPI and generated TypeScript client regenerate cleanly and pass contract checks.
Pagination/filter validation and RBAC constraints are covered by API tests.
excludedApproaches: Backend route changes without OpenAPI/client updates.
Temporary frontend calls bypassing generated client contracts.
executionConstraints: Add all phase-2 missing endpoints in API and OpenAPI spec together.
Regenerate `contract/generated/ts-client/` for every contract change.
Keep endpoint semantics aligned with existing domain state machine and authorization model.
features.name: API Flow Gap Closure and Contract Sync
features.rationale: Frontend implementation cannot proceed safely without complete contract surface.
features.summary: Implement missing API workflows and keep contract artifacts in lockstep.

acceptanceSignals: SvelteKit hooks inject authenticated actor context for all role routes.
Access token refresh works (15 min access, 7 day refresh) with safe re-login behavior.
Unauthorized cross-role route access is blocked both server-side and client-side.
excludedApproaches: Hardcoding identities inside page components.
Auth implementation that cannot swap providers cleanly.
executionConstraints: Define pluggable auth provider interface with mock provider default in dev.
Centralize JWT/session lifecycle in middleware/hooks; avoid duplicated auth logic in pages.
Preserve clean seam for real OIDC/Vendor MFA providers.
features.name: Authentication and Session Middleware Foundation
features.rationale: Role-safe routing and API access are required before user-facing pages.
features.summary: Implement provider-agnostic authn/authz pipeline with mock-first runtime support.

acceptanceSignals: Single SvelteKit app serves `/employee/*`, `/vendor/*`, and `/admin/*` route trees with role-aware navigation.
Centralized i18n message catalog (zh-TW) is used for UI strings and API error translation.
Global loading, retry-safe action states, and offline messaging are present.
excludedApproaches: Handwritten backend `fetch` calls in feature pages.
Scattered hardcoded Chinese strings across components.
executionConstraints: Use SvelteKit 2.x + Tailwind 4.x + TypeScript.
Use generated TS client as the only backend API access layer.
Keep responsive baseline: mobile-first employee UX, desktop-first vendor/admin without tablet breakage.
features.name: Shared Frontend Platform and UX Guardrails
features.rationale: A stable shared UI foundation prevents duplicated logic across three role portals.
features.summary: Create common frontend architecture, routing, localization, and reliability primitives.

acceptanceSignals: Vendor can upload valid image/doc artifacts and obtain usable object references.
Invalid MIME and oversize uploads are rejected with localized errors.
Menu image and compliance document retrieval obeys bucket access policy and audit expectations.
excludedApproaches: Unbounded binary/base64 payloads through normal API bodies.
Direct public write permissions to storage buckets.
executionConstraints: Implement presigned upload flow and metadata persistence for menu images/documents/exports.
Enforce MIME allowlist (`image/jpeg`, `image/png`, `image/webp`) and selected size policy from ISS-001.
Generate thumbnail variants for menu images and persist object keys, not opaque large blobs (unless ISS-001 decides DB path).
features.name: Object Storage Upload and Retrieval Pipeline
features.rationale: Vendor menu/document workflows and admin review depend on file storage.
features.summary: Integrate S3-compatible object storage for images, compliance files, and exports.

acceptanceSignals: Employee can complete flow: login -> browse menus -> place/modify order -> view timeline -> pickup verification.
Payroll summary and dispute submission flows are functional and stateful.
Role and plant filtering behavior matches API scope controls.
excludedApproaches: Client-only authorization assumptions without server enforcement.
Temporary bypass of generated client for speed.
executionConstraints: Implement menu calendar/week views, filters, stock-aware ordering, and cutoff countdown behavior.
Render TOTP pickup QR with 30-second refresh cadence and clear mobile readability.
Expose order list/status mappings and payroll detail/dispute UX in localized form.
features.name: Employee Frontend MVP
features.rationale: Employee ordering and pickup are the primary end-to-end value path.
features.summary: Deliver complete employee-side browser workflow for ordering and payroll visibility.

acceptanceSignals: Cache hit path is observable for menu/mapping reads and reduces DB read pressure.
Concurrent stock decrement tests prevent oversell.
JetStream consumers process idempotently and route repeated failures to DLQ.
excludedApproaches: Treating cache as source of truth for order correctness.
At-most-once assumptions for event processing.
executionConstraints: Implement Valkey key patterns/TTLs for menu, delivery mapping, stock, session, and lock use-cases.
Apply explicit cache invalidation on menu/mapping writes.
Provision JetStream streams/consumers with backpressure (`max_ack_pending`) and DLQ policy after 5 failures.
features.name: Cache and Event Backbone (Valkey + NATS JetStream)
features.rationale: High-frequency reads and async workflows must be decoupled before vendor/admin heavy operations.
features.summary: Add cache consistency and event-driven workers for fulfillment, notifications, settlement, and audit.

acceptanceSignals: Vendor can run daily cycle: manage menu/status -> inspect fulfillment board -> export batch -> update delivery status.
Vendor document upload/list and review status visibility are complete.
Order and revenue views support required filters and monthly summaries.
excludedApproaches: Mixing admin-only operations into vendor role routes.
Browser-only export generation without tracked backend batch records.
executionConstraints: Support menu CRUD-like operations, status patch, temporary sold-out action, and ordering window configuration.
Integrate fulfillment board, print/PDF export, and delivery status updates with batch tracking.
Keep vendor UX desktop-first while preserving tablet operability.
features.name: Vendor Frontend MVP
features.rationale: Supply-side execution is required for end-to-end order fulfillment.
features.summary: Deliver vendor self-service menu, fulfillment, delivery, and operational dashboards.

acceptanceSignals: Admin can review vendors/documents, manage mapping matrix, and execute settlement/dispute workflows.
Anomaly board supports assign -> investigate -> remediate -> close with audit trail.
Audit and operations dashboards support required filters and date/plant slicing.
excludedApproaches: Skipping review reason capture on compliance decisions.
Settlement exports without deterministic schema/version tracking.
executionConstraints: Implement compliance review lifecycle, document expiry board, and suspend/restore reason capture.
Implement settlement cycle lock/unlock, payroll export, exception triage, and dispute assignment/status.
Provide anomaly rule management and SLA status rendering according to resolved threshold policy.
features.name: Committee Admin Frontend MVP
features.rationale: Operational governance and compliance handling are core business controls.
features.summary: Deliver admin-side compliance, mapping, settlement, anomaly, audit, and analytics workflows.

acceptanceSignals: Gateway routes API/MCP/frontend/health paths with TLS and policy controls.
Default-deny NetworkPolicy is effective and allows only approved traffic matrix.
Sensitive runtime configuration comes via ExternalSecret-managed Kubernetes Secrets.
excludedApproaches: Flat allow-all pod networking.
Embedding secrets directly in manifests or repository files.
executionConstraints: Use Gateway API for host/path routing, TLS via cert-manager, and configured rate limits/CORS/body-size policies.
Implement per-component NetworkPolicy allowlists from defined communication matrix.
Adopt External Secrets Operator flow for DB, queue, storage, OIDC, TOTP, and JWT keys with rotation windows.
features.name: Edge Routing, Network Isolation, and Secret Management
features.rationale: Secure, production-usable runtime boundaries are mandatory before rollout.
features.summary: Establish gateway security controls, zero-trust networking, and externalized secret flow.

acceptanceSignals: `kustomize build` succeeds for dev/staging/production overlays.
HPA/KEDA objects exist with configured metrics and bounds per component class.
Topology spread constraints and HA placement rules are present for critical stateful/stateless services.
excludedApproaches: Single-environment manifests without overlay separation.
Manual scaling-only operations outside declarative manifests.
executionConstraints: Create `base`, `components`, and environment overlays (`dev`, `staging`, `production`).
Implement HPA/KEDA scaling profiles and topology spread/multi-AZ constraints per requirement.
Keep dev overlay lightweight and debuggable while preserving structural parity with staging/prod.
features.name: Kustomize Environment Layers and Autoscaling Topology
features.rationale: Repeatable promotion and scaling policy require environment-specific declarative configuration.
features.summary: Codify deployment overlays, scaling policy, and topology constraints.

acceptanceSignals: New workflows exist and pass: image build, migration check, e2e smoke, production deploy, load-test gate.
Migration checks validate reversibility and fail PRs on non-reversible changes.
Production deploy path consumes kustomize production overlay as the source artifact.
excludedApproaches: Manual production kubectl operations without pipeline evidence.
Skipping smoke/load gates before production rollout.
executionConstraints: Add GitHub Actions workflows for image build, DB migration checks, E2E smoke, deployment, and load-test gate.
Use staging/ephemeral deploy targets for smoke and performance checks.
Keep CI gates contract-aware and environment-layer aware.
features.name: CI/CD Expansion and Release Gates
features.rationale: Operational safety and repeatability depend on automated quality gates.
features.summary: Extend pipelines to enforce build, migration, e2e, deploy, and load-test checks.

acceptanceSignals: Employee/admin login succeeds against Corporate OIDC in staging with claim mapping.
Vendor MFA login flow succeeds and issues valid session tokens.
Feature flags can disable real providers outside production while keeping middleware contract unchanged.
excludedApproaches: Shipping production auth with mock-only identities.
Bypassing issuer/audience/signature validation.
executionConstraints: Implement real provider adapters behind feature flags (e.g., `auth.oidc.enabled`, `auth.vendor_mfa.enabled`).
Consume client secrets and signing keys via external secret pipeline only.
Preserve mock mode for local development, not as production fallback behavior.
features.name: Real Identity Provider Integration (OIDC + Vendor MFA)
features.rationale: External identity integration may be dependency-bound but should be planned as a clean extension.
features.summary: Wire real enterprise auth providers without changing core middleware contracts.

acceptanceSignals: k6 staging tests meet agreed throughput/latency/error budgets with evidence artifact.
Autoscaling behavior (HPA/KEDA) reaches expected ranges and stabilizes under load.
Failover/backpressure drills confirm resilience targets (including DB failover objective).
excludedApproaches: Declaring scale readiness without measured benchmarks.
Branching application logic per environment instead of scaling policy controls.
executionConstraints: Run staged capacity tests and tune manifests/queue settings without introducing code-path forks.
Validate queue throughput, cache hit behavior, and DB read/write separation under stress.
Ship scale profile adjustments as declarative config + documented benchmark evidence.
features.name: Capacity Validation and Scale Tuning
features.rationale: Hyperscale targets require empirical validation after functional completeness.
features.summary: Benchmark and tune system toward target RPS/SLO with repeatable evidence.

options.label: Object storage only
options.rationale: Aligns with scale, security, presigned upload, and backup strategy in §12.6.

options.label: DB base64 only
options.rationale: Simpler MVP implementation but conflicts with large-scale architecture and storage separation.

options.label: Dual mode by environment
options.rationale: Technically possible but introduces dual source-of-truth and legacy-like maintenance overhead.

clarifications.prompt: MVP 的檔案與圖片儲存唯一來源要採哪個？
clarifications.rationale: Current requirement text defines two competing canonical storage paths and size limits.
clarifications.recommendationSummary: Choose object storage as the only source-of-truth, and remove DB base64 path from runtime requirements.

issues.details: §9 allows base64-in-DB (<=500KB) for MVP, while §12.6 mandates object storage presigned flow with <=2MB and thumbnails. This is a direct source-of-truth conflict for upload architecture.
issues.title: Image/File Storage Path Conflict (DB base64 vs Object Storage)

options.label: Mock-only for this phase
options.rationale: Maximizes delivery predictability; real provider hookup deferred to optional feature.

options.label: Real OIDC now, Vendor MFA later
options.rationale: Balances enterprise readiness with reduced external dependency risk.

options.label: Full real OIDC + Vendor MFA now
options.rationale: Most complete but highest schedule risk due external integration dependencies.

clarifications.prompt: 第二階段驗收是否必須接通真實 Corporate OIDC 與 Vendor MFA？
clarifications.rationale: Auth integration depth changes acceptance scope and sequencing for F05/F15.
clarifications.recommendationSummary: Lock one explicit phase gate now to prevent auth implementation churn.

issues.details: Spec allows mock auth for MVP but also defines full real auth integration. Acceptance depth is not explicitly locked.
issues.title: Authentication Scope for Phase-2 Acceptance

options.label: CSV only for MVP
options.rationale: Fastest path with deterministic schema control and easy downstream parsing.

options.label: XLSX only for MVP
options.rationale: Useful when HR demands template fidelity but adds generation complexity.

options.label: CSV + XLSX both in MVP
options.rationale: Covers both consumption modes with higher implementation/test cost.

clarifications.prompt: 月結扣款匯出 MVP 要交付哪種格式？
clarifications.rationale: Admin settlement export cannot be finalized without locked output format and schema expectation.
clarifications.recommendationSummary: Default to CSV-first unless HR explicitly requires XLSX in phase-2.

issues.details: Requirement asks for HR-compatible payroll export (CSV/Excel) but does not lock MVP format/schema versioning expectations.
issues.title: Payroll Export Format and Contract Not Locked

options.label: In-app notifications
options.rationale: Lowest complexity, fully in-product, supports MVP visibility requirements.

options.label: Email notifications
options.rationale: Adds out-of-app reach for sold-out/rush reminders.

options.label: Web push notifications
options.rationale: Real-time delivery with browser permission flow and additional infra.

clarifications.prompt: MVP 通知通道要包含哪些？
clarifications.rationale: Channel scope affects event payloads, worker implementation, and consent UX.
clarifications.recommendationSummary: Start with in-app channel, then add email/web-push as optional extensions.

issues.details: Employee flow mentions push alerts or prominent UI prompts, but required notification channels are not explicitly fixed.
issues.title: Notification Channel Scope Ambiguity

options.label: Self-host operators in-cluster
options.rationale: Maximizes IaC reproducibility across environments.

options.label: Hybrid: dev self-host, prod managed
options.rationale: Common enterprise pattern balancing control and operational burden.

options.label: Managed services everywhere possible
options.rationale: Reduces ops toil but shifts repo focus from infra manifests to integration config.

clarifications.prompt: 正式環境基礎設施策略要採哪種？
clarifications.rationale: Operator-vs-managed choice changes manifest ownership and delivery artifacts in F12/F13/F16.
clarifications.recommendationSummary: Lock one environment strategy early to avoid rewriting infra overlays.

issues.details: Multiple alternatives are listed (CloudNativePG vs managed DB, operator vs managed cache/queue/storage, Envoy vs Cilium gateway) without environment-level lock.
issues.title: Infrastructure Deployment Model Not Fixed

options.label: UUIDv7
options.rationale: Strong temporal locality and straightforward PostgreSQL handling.

options.label: ULID
options.rationale: Lexicographically sortable and language-agnostic identifier format.

clarifications.prompt: 全域主鍵格式採 UUIDv7 還是 ULID？
clarifications.rationale: Mixed ID schemes complicate schema conventions, indexing, and API typing.
clarifications.recommendationSummary: Use UUIDv7 as default unless ULID is a hard organizational standard.

issues.details: Requirement allows UUIDv7 or ULID but does not choose one global standard.
issues.title: Primary Key Standard Not Chosen

options.label: Severity defaults + per-rule override
options.rationale: Provides consistent baseline with controlled flexibility.

options.label: Per-rule custom only
options.rationale: Maximum flexibility but weaker cross-rule comparability.

options.label: Label-only (no breach automation)
options.rationale: Lowest complexity but weak operational governance signal.

clarifications.prompt: 異常告警 SLA 的門檻計算要採哪種模式？
clarifications.rationale: Admin anomaly board status (normal/risk/breach) needs deterministic thresholds to be testable.
clarifications.recommendationSummary: Use severity defaults with optional per-rule overrides.

issues.details: Anomaly workflow requires SLA status rendering, but threshold formulas and timing baselines are unspecified.
issues.title: Anomaly SLA Threshold Policy Undefined

options.label: Keep 25k RPS target for phase-2 gate
options.rationale: Aligns with documented headroom target and infrastructure sizing.

options.label: Use lower staged target now, ramp later
options.rationale: Reduces immediate risk while preserving measurable progress.

options.label: Defer hard RPS gate to post-MVP
options.rationale: Focuses phase-2 on functional completeness only.

clarifications.prompt: 第二階段是否要以 25k RPS 作為硬性驗收門檻？
clarifications.rationale: Capacity gate strictness changes delivery risk and release readiness criteria.
clarifications.recommendationSummary: Adopt staged capacity gates unless business requires immediate 25k RPS certification.

issues.details: Throughput assumptions are explicit but may diverge from actual usage and environment limits; staged benchmarking policy is not locked.

activation.rationale: API and frontend contract drift can break every role portal.

checks: API behavior changes include synchronized updates to OpenAPI and generated TS client artifacts.
Contract verification workflow passes on each PR touching API surface.
Frontend code imports generated client modules for backend calls instead of handwritten API wrappers.

reinforcements.instructions: Treat OpenAPI as the executable interface source-of-truth and enforce lockstep regeneration.
reinforcements.name: Contract-First Delivery Gate
reinforcements.policy: No contract drift between backend handlers, OpenAPI spec, and generated TypeScript client.

activation.rationale: Persistence features are high-risk for irreversible data defects.

checks: Migration up/down reversibility test passes in CI using PostgreSQL container.
Schema review confirms BIGINT money, UTC timestamps, and append-only event tables.
Integration tests run on real PostgreSQL, not DB mocks, for transactional flows.

reinforcements.instructions: Accept schema/query changes only through migration-reviewed, test-backed SQL paths.
reinforcements.name: Persistence Integrity Gate
reinforcements.policy: All durable data behavior must be migration-driven, query-auditable, and integration-tested.

activation.rationale: Role boundaries and token lifecycle are central to all UIs and APIs.

checks: Route guard tests prove `/employee/*`, `/vendor/*`, `/admin/*` cannot be crossed by wrong roles.
Token expiration/refresh scenarios are covered with positive and negative tests.
Backend authorization checks validate issuer/audience/claims before privileged actions.

reinforcements.instructions: Centralize authn/authz in middleware/hooks and enforce role scopes consistently.
reinforcements.name: Authentication and Authorization Boundary Gate
reinforcements.policy: Every protected route and endpoint must enforce actor identity, role, and scope deterministically.

activation.rationale: Upload flows affect security, compliance, and operational cost.

checks: Upload API validates MIME type and size limits before accepting metadata.
Bucket access model is verified per artifact class (public read vs private read).
Object references stored in DB are auditable and can be revoked/expired per policy.

reinforcements.instructions: Enforce one storage source-of-truth, explicit validation, and auditable access controls.
reinforcements.name: File Storage Safety Gate
reinforcements.policy: Uploaded artifacts must follow a single canonical storage path with strict validation and access controls.

activation.rationale: Cache/event layers can silently corrupt state if consistency is weak.

checks: Cache invalidation tests confirm read-after-write correctness for menu and mapping updates.
Consumer replay tests prove idempotent processing under at-least-once delivery.
DLQ routing and alerting trigger after configured retry exhaustion.

reinforcements.instructions: Keep DB authoritative, make event handlers idempotent, and verify failure-path observability.
reinforcements.name: Cache and Event Consistency Gate
reinforcements.policy: Performance optimizations must not compromise correctness under concurrency or retries.

activation.rationale: Usability failures directly block operational adoption.

checks: All async actions show loading state and prevent duplicate submissions.
API/network errors are translated into understandable zh-TW user messages.
Responsive checks cover employee mobile and vendor/admin desktop-tablet layouts.

reinforcements.instructions: Fail UI reviews when loading/error/offline states or role-specific responsiveness are missing.
reinforcements.name: User Experience Reliability Gate
reinforcements.policy: Every async user action must be resilient, localized, and device-appropriate.

activation.rationale: Security posture depends on runtime isolation and secret hygiene.

checks: Gateway policy enforces TLS, CORS allowlist, request-size limits, and endpoint rate limits.
NetworkPolicy verification confirms default-deny plus explicit allowlist matrix only.
Secret manifests reference ExternalSecret flows; plaintext credentials are absent from repo.

reinforcements.instructions: Treat edge policy, network isolation, and external secret sync as release blockers.
reinforcements.name: Zero-Trust Runtime Gate
reinforcements.policy: Production runtime must enforce least-privilege traffic and externally managed secrets.

activation.rationale: Scale and reliability claims must be test-backed before promotion.

checks: Staging smoke tests pass before production promotion.
Load-test gate publishes throughput/latency/error evidence for target profile.
Failover and scaling behavior are documented and reproducible from CI artifacts.

reinforcements.instructions: Use measured benchmark evidence to tune scaling policies and finalize release readiness.
reinforcements.name: Performance Readiness Gate
reinforcements.policy: Capacity and resiliency assertions require repeatable benchmark and drill evidence.

activation.rationale: Project explicitly rejects legacy compatibility and unverified fallback paths.

checks: Legacy in-memory production path is removed after persistence cutover.
Optional capabilities are isolated behind explicit feature flags rather than parallel permanent paths.
No unverified fallback logic is introduced in critical auth/storage/order flows.

reinforcements.instructions: Continuously remove obsolete compatibility branches and keep one clear runtime path per capability.
reinforcements.name: No-Legacy Path Enforcement
reinforcements.policy: Prefer clean, singular, verified implementations over compatibility-heavy branching.

roles.name: Employee
responsibilities: Browse plant-scoped menus and complete pre-order workflows before cutoff.
Track order lifecycle, pickup status, and payroll deduction/refund details.
Submit and monitor deduction disputes and reminder preferences.

roles.name: Vendor Operator
responsibilities: Manage menu items, status, supply limits, and ordering windows.
Run fulfillment board operations, export batches/labels, and update delivery states.
Upload compliance documents and monitor order/revenue views.

roles.name: Committee Admin
responsibilities: Review vendor compliance, approve/reject/remediate documents, and manage suspension actions.
Control vendor-plant mappings, settlement cycles, exception handling, and dispute assignment.
Operate anomaly governance, audit investigations, and operations analytics dashboards.

roles.name: HR/Payroll Consumer
responsibilities: Consume payroll deduction exports and reconcile monthly settlement outputs.
Report schema/format mismatches and downstream integration exceptions.

roles.name: Backend Engineer
responsibilities: Implement SQLx persistence, transactional domain flows, and API endpoint behavior.
Maintain OpenAPI lockstep with server handlers and generated client artifacts.
Build queue consumers/workers with idempotency and DLQ behavior.

roles.name: Frontend Engineer
responsibilities: Build SvelteKit role portals and shared UI primitives with Tailwind and TypeScript.
Integrate generated TS client, route guards, localization, and resilient async UX.
Deliver employee/vendor/admin workflows and responsive behavior targets.

roles.name: Platform/SRE Engineer
responsibilities: Own Kubernetes manifests, overlays, autoscaling, and topology constraints.
Deploy and operate gateway, poolers, cache, queue, and storage runtime components.
Maintain CI/CD deployment gates and environment promotion reliability.

roles.name: Security and IAM Engineer
responsibilities: Define token validation, RBAC enforcement, and auth provider integration controls.
Design secret rotation, dual-key windows, and external secret governance.
Review network isolation and edge policy configuration for least privilege.

roles.name: QA and Automation Engineer
responsibilities: Implement integration/e2e/contract/load test suites and release gate evidence.
Validate critical end-to-end flows across employee, vendor, and admin journeys.
Track regression risk across schema, auth, and event-driven processing changes.

roles.name: Product/Delivery Lead
responsibilities: Lock scope decisions on open clarifications and sequence backlog execution.
Define acceptance criteria for MVP and optional hardening milestones.
Coordinate external dependencies (IdP, HR format, managed service constraints).

snapshot.summary: Phase-2 planning is structured as 14 core features for runnable MVP + secure deployability, followed by 2 optional hardening features (real provider auth and capacity certification). One issue is currently truly blocking (storage source-of-truth conflict); the remaining open items are decision gates that can be resolved in parallel without stopping core implementation.
